prompt: An open incident has widespread business impact and requires coordinated response beyond routine incident handling. What should happen first?
choices: Initiate major incident procedures with accelerated response and stakeholder communications
Convert every related incident to a Standard change template without approval
Close all open incidents as duplicates without agent review
Disable SLA and inactivity monitor tracking for the assignment group
explanation: Major incidents use a separate high-priority procedure with shorter timescales, resource coordination, and communication plans for significant business disruption.

prompt: When a problem coordinator selects Fix during root cause investigation, what happens to the problem record?
choices: The problem state changes to Fix in Progress so a change request can implement the permanent fix
The problem is automatically closed with Risk accepted as the resolution code
All related incidents are resolved without entering resolution information
The problem returns directly to New and skips further investigation
explanation: Fix moves the problem to Fix in Progress so the coordinator can create or link change requests to apply the permanent resolution.

prompt: When a problem has no permanent fix and the organization accepts the residual risk, which action is appropriate on the problem record?
choices: Click Accept Risk to close or resolve the problem with a Risk accepted resolution code
Create an Emergency change request from every related incident automatically
Mark Duplicate and move related records to a catalog Requested Item
Disable the Update Problem State to Assess business rule permanently
explanation: Accept Risk closes or resolves the problem when no permanent fix is available and the organization formally accepts the remaining risk.

prompt: Why would a problem coordinator associate multiple configuration items with a problem record?
choices: To identify all CIs affected by the same underlying problem issue
To replace CMDB identification rules for the cmdb_ci class entirely
To auto-generate Standard change templates for every affected user
To bypass the Assess and Root Cause Analysis states on the problem
explanation: Affected and Impacted Services/CIs related lists document every configuration item impacted by the same root issue for analysis and remediation.

prompt: After a permanent fix is verified on a problem record, what sequence completes the problem lifecycle?
choices: Click Resolve to move the problem to Resolved, then Complete to close it with Fix Applied as the resolution code
Click Accept Risk immediately without documenting any resolution notes
Mark Duplicate to merge the problem into a catalog Requested Item record
Revert the linked change request to New without post-implementation review
explanation: Resolve records Fix Applied in the Resolved state; Complete then moves the problem to Closed with the same resolution code.
